key_evidence: HHS OIG report (surfaced Jun 12-15 2026) found Humana denied 72% of long-term care hospital (LTCH) admission requests and 54% of inpatient rehabilitation facility (IRF) requests under Medicare Advantage — far above the ~42% peer average.
Across the three largest MA insurers, ~95% of appealed skilled-nursing denials were overturned, indicating most initial denials were not clinically warranted.
Post-acute LTCH/IRF beneficiaries are among the highest-need, lowest-power patients, making the inverse-prioritization pattern an acute equity (EQU) and action (ACT) failure.
Partial mitigant: Humana publicly committed to reduce Medicare Advantage prior authorizations in 2026 — a documented but unproven correction signal that keeps ACC off the floor.

evidence.quote: the three biggest MA insurers by enrollment — UnitedHealth, Humana and CVS — received the most admission requests, and denied them at a much higher rate than the majority of their peers.
evidence.claim: Humana, as one of the three largest MA insurers, denied post-acute admission requests at a much higher rate than most peers.
evidence.source: Healthcare Dive (reporting on HHS OIG report)

evidence.quote: Humana's LTCH denial rate was 72%, while UnitedHealth Group had a denial rate of 71%.
evidence.claim: Humana denied 72% of long-term care hospital admission requests, second-highest among the three largest MA insurers.

evidence.quote: The IRF denial rate for UnitedHealth Group was the highest at 66%, compared to 54% for Humana and 51% for CVS Health.
evidence.claim: Humana denied 54% of inpatient rehabilitation facility admission requests.
